Module com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java
Package com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java

Interface JavaRequirements

All Superinterfaces:
Configuration
All Known Implementing Classes:
DefaultJavaRequirements
public interface JavaRequirements
extends Configuration
Verifies the requirements of types from the Java core API.

Implementations must be thread-safe.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type Method Description
    ArrayVerifier<boolean[],​Boolean> assertThat​(boolean[] actual, String name)
    Same as requireThat(boolean[], String) but does nothing if assertions are disabled.
    PrimitiveBooleanVerifier assertThat​(boolean actual, String name)
    Same as requireThat(boolean, String) but does nothing if assertions are disabled for this class.
    ArrayVerifier<byte[],​Byte> assertThat​(byte[] actual, String name)
    Same as requireThat(byte[], String) but does nothing if assertions are disabled.

    JavaRequirements putContext​(String name, Object value)
    Adds or updates contextual information associated with the exception message.
    JavaRequirements removeContext​(String name)
    Removes contextual information associated with the exception message.
    ArrayVerifier<boolean[],​Boolean> requireThat​(boolean[] actual, String name)
    Verifies the requirements of a primitive boolean array.

    <T> ObjectValidator<T> validateThat​(T actual, String name)
    Validates the requirements of an Object.
    JavaRequirements withAssertionsDisabled()
    Indicates that assertThat() shouldn't do anything.
    JavaRequirements withAssertionsEnabled()
    Indicates that assertThat() should invoke requireThat().
    JavaRequirements withCleanStackTrace()
    Indicates that exception stack traces should omit references to this library.
    JavaRequirements withConfiguration​(Configuration configuration)
    Replaces a verifier's configuration.
    JavaRequirements withDiff()
    Indicates that exceptions should show the difference between the actual and expected values.
    JavaRequirements withoutCleanStackTrace()
    Indicates that exception stack traces should contain references to this library.
    JavaRequirements withoutDiff()
    Indicates that exceptions should not show the difference between the actual and expected values.
    <T> JavaRequirements withoutStringConverter​(Class<T> type)
    Indicates that an object's toString() method should be used to convert it to a String.
    <T> JavaRequirements withStringConverter​(Class<T> type, Function<T,​String> converter)
    Indicates that a function should be used to convert an object to a String.

    Methods declared in interface com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java.Configuration

    assertionsAreEnabled, getContext, isCleanStackTrace, isDiffEnabled, toString

  • Method Details

    • putContext

      JavaRequirements putContext​(String name, Object value)
      Description copied from interface: Configuration
      Adds or updates contextual information associated with the exception message. Overrides any values associated with the name at the ThreadRequirements level.
      Specified by:
      putContext in interface Configuration
      Parameters:
      name - the name of the parameter
      value - the value of the parameter
      Returns:
      the updated configuration

    • removeContext

      JavaRequirements removeContext​(String name)
      Description copied from interface: Configuration
      Removes contextual information associated with the exception message.
      Specified by:
      removeContext in interface Configuration
      Parameters:
      name - the name of the parameter
      Returns:
      the updated configuration

    • withAssertionsDisabled

      JavaRequirements withAssertionsDisabled()
      Description copied from interface: Configuration
      Indicates that assertThat() shouldn't do anything.
      Specified by:
      withAssertionsDisabled in interface Configuration
      Returns:
      the updated configuration

    • withAssertionsEnabled

      JavaRequirements withAssertionsEnabled()
      Description copied from interface: Configuration
      Indicates that assertThat() should invoke requireThat().
      Specified by:
      withAssertionsEnabled in interface Configuration
      Returns:
      the updated configuration

    • withCleanStackTrace

      JavaRequirements withCleanStackTrace()
      Description copied from interface: Configuration
      Indicates that exception stack traces should omit references to this library.
      Specified by:
      withCleanStackTrace in interface Configuration
      Returns:
      the updated configuration
      See Also:
      Configuration.isCleanStackTrace()

    • withoutCleanStackTrace

      JavaRequirements withoutCleanStackTrace()
      Description copied from interface: Configuration
      Indicates that exception stack traces should contain references to this library.
      Specified by:
      withoutCleanStackTrace in interface Configuration
      Returns:
      the updated configuration
      See Also:
      Configuration.isCleanStackTrace()

    • withDiff

      JavaRequirements withDiff()
      Description copied from interface: Configuration
      Indicates that exceptions should show the difference between the actual and expected values.
      Specified by:
      withDiff in interface Configuration
      Returns:
      the updated configuration

    • withoutDiff

      JavaRequirements withoutDiff()
      Description copied from interface: Configuration
      Indicates that exceptions should not show the difference between the actual and expected values.
      Specified by:
      withoutDiff in interface Configuration
      Returns:
      the updated configuration

    • withStringConverter

      <T> JavaRequirements withStringConverter​(Class<T> type, Function<T,​String> converter)
      Description copied from interface: Configuration
      Indicates that a function should be used to convert an object to a String.

      Please note that type must be an exact match (e.g. configuring a converter for Set will not match values of type HashSet).

      Specified by:
      withStringConverter in interface Configuration
      Type Parameters:
      T - the type of object being converted
      Parameters:
      type - the type of object being converted (non-primitive arrays are mapped to Object[].class)
      converter - a function that converts an object of the specified type to a String
      Returns:
      the updated configuration

    • withoutStringConverter

      <T> JavaRequirements withoutStringConverter​(Class<T> type)
      Description copied from interface: Configuration
      Indicates that an object's toString() method should be used to convert it to a String.
      Specified by:
      withoutStringConverter in interface Configuration
      Type Parameters:
      T - the type of object being converted
      Parameters:
      type - the type of object being converted
      Returns:
      the updated configuration

    • withConfiguration

      JavaRequirements withConfiguration​(Configuration configuration)
      Description copied from interface: Configuration
      Replaces a verifier's configuration.
      Specified by:
      withConfiguration in interface Configuration
      Parameters:
      configuration - a new configuration
      Returns:
      the updated configuration

    • requireThat

      <T> ObjectVerifier<T> requireThat​(T actual, String name)
      Verifies the requirements of an Object.
      Type Parameters:
      T - the type of the value
      Parameters:
      actual - the actual value
      name - the name of the value
      Returns:
      a verifier for the value
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if name is null
      IllegalArgumentException - if name is empty

    • assertThat

      <T> ObjectVerifier<T> assertThat​(T actual, String name)
      Same as requireThat(Object, String) but does nothing if assertions are disabled.
      Type Parameters:
      T - the type of the value
      Parameters:
      actual - the actual value
      name - the name of the value
      Returns:
      a verifier for the value
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if name is null
      IllegalArgumentException - if name is empty

    • validateThat

      <T> ObjectValidator<T> validateThat​(T actual, String name)
      Validates the requirements of an Object.
      Type Parameters:
      T - the type of the value
      Parameters:
      actual - the actual value
      name - the name of the value
      Returns:
      a validator for the value
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if name is null
      IllegalArgumentException - if name is empty

    • requireThat

      <C extends Collection<E>,​ E> CollectionVerifier<C,​E> requireThat​(C actual, String name)
      Verifies the requirements of a Collection.
      Type Parameters:
      C - the type of the collection
      E - the type of elements in the collection
      Parameters:
      actual - the actual value
      name - the name of the value
      Returns:
      a verifier for the value
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if name is null
      IllegalArgumentException - if name is empty

      IllegalArgumentException - if name is empty

    • validateThat

      Parameters:
      actual - the actual value
      name - the name of the value
      Returns:
      a validator for the value
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if name is null
      IllegalArgumentException - if name is empty

    • assertThat

      StringVerifier assertThat​(String actual, String name)
      Same as requireThat(String, String) but does nothing if assertions are disabled.
      Parameters:
      actual - the actual value
      name - the name of the value
      Returns:
      a verifier for the value
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if name is null
      IllegalArgumentException - if name is empty

    • validateThat

      StringValidator validateThat​(String actual, String name)
      Validates the requirements of a String.
      Parameters:
      actual - the actual value
      name - the name of the value
      Returns:
      a validator for the value
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if name is null
      IllegalArgumentException - if name is empty
